rationale: The cited paper appears only in the limitations/future research discussion, where the authors note that trade-off aversion has been studied with cognitive neuroscience methods and suggest similar methods for future trade-in research. It is not used to build the main theoretical model, derive hypotheses, justify measures, or interpret the core empirical findings. The central argument and tests in the citing paper are grounded in prospect theory and prior trade-in/partitioned-price work, not in this specific fMRI attraction-effect study.
